model_access.name: Codestral
model_access.summary: Mistral's code-specialized open model line for self-hosted coding workflows.
constraints: consumer GPU use is realistic only with enough VRAM or quantization
primarily valuable for coding rather than general-purpose multimodal use
not a standalone chat product by itself

model_access.name: DeepSeek V3 / R1
model_access.summary: DeepSeek's open model family spanning large general-purpose and reasoning-oriented self-hosted models.
constraints: the full flagship models need serious hardware
local use usually depends on distilled or quantized variants
organizational policy may restrict use based on provider origin

model_access.name: Leanstral
model_access.summary: Mistral's open-source code agent specialized for Lean 4 proof engineering. Apache 2.0 weights with MCP tool-use training. Also available via the Mistral Labs API endpoint `labs-leanstral-2603` and agent mode in Mistral Vibe.
constraints: specialized for Lean 4 formal verification, not general-purpose coding
Labs API endpoint may be time-limited
consumer GPU use depends on model size and quantization

model_access.name: Llama 3.3
model_access.summary: Meta's open-weight Llama 3.3 family for self-hosted general-purpose use.
constraints: higher-end variants still need significant VRAM
practical local use often depends on quantized builds
not a packaged end-user product by itself

model_access.name: Llama 4
model_access.summary: Meta's Llama 4 family extends local/open model access into multimodal and very large-context self-hosted use.
constraints: model size and context claims can exceed ordinary consumer hardware
license terms differ from permissive Apache-style models
multimodal support depends on runtime compatibility

model_access.name: Mistral Large / Nemo
model_access.summary: Mistral's open model family ranges from consumer-friendly Nemo to much larger self-hosted models approaching frontier quality.
constraints: runtime practicality depends heavily on which model size is chosen
larger variants need serious GPU capacity
the model family should not be treated as a single hardware profile

model_access.name: Mistral Small 3
model_access.summary: Mistral Small 3 is a locally runnable open model line focused on strong general performance at more achievable hardware sizes.
constraints: consumer use is realistic but still depends on memory and quantization choices
capabilities change across minor versions
performance claims depend on runtime and hardware

model_access.name: Mistral Small 4
model_access.summary: Mistral Small 4 is a 119B Mixture-of-Experts model (128 experts, 4 active per token, ~6B active parameters) that unifies instruct, reasoning, multimodal, and agentic coding into a single open model. Apache 2.0 license. 256k context window with native vision and a configurable `reasoning_effort` parameter. Available via Mistral API and self-hosted, but requires datacenter-class GPUs (4× H100 minimum).
constraints: datacenter-class hardware required: minimum 4× H100 or 2× H200 or 1× DGX B200
NOT practical for consumer GPUs despite being open-weight
119B total parameters but only ~6B active per token (MoE)
FP8/NVFP4 support recommended for optimal performance

model_access.name: Qwen 2.5
model_access.summary: Alibaba's Qwen 2.5 family offers a broad range of self-hosted model sizes from lightweight local use to much larger deployments.
constraints: capability and hardware requirements vary substantially by size
organizational policy may restrict use based on provider origin
the family includes both practical local sizes and much larger variants

model_access.name: Qwen 3
model_access.summary: Alibaba's Qwen 3 family extends local/open model access with a wide size range and hybrid thinking behaviors.
constraints: model sizes range from practical local options to very large MoE deployments
reasoning-style behaviors may depend on runtime or prompt controls
the family is still model access, not a standalone product experience

model_access.name: Qwen-Coder
model_access.summary: Qwen-Coder is Alibaba's code-focused open model line for self-hosted coding tasks.
constraints: tuned for coding rather than broad multimodal use
local performance depends on model size and quantization
not a packaged end-user coding product by itself
